"""Integration tests for pipeline routes."""

import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

from app.models.video import VideoProject, VideoStatus


class TestTriggerPipeline:
    """Test POST /api/v1/pipeline endpoint."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_trigger_pipeline_success\(self, client: AsyncClient, db_session, mocker\):
        """Test successful pipeline trigger."""
        # Mock Celery task dispatch
        mock_celery_result = MagicMock\(\)
        mock_celery_result.id = "celery-task-123"

        mock_delay = mocker.patch\(
            "app.api.routes.pipeline.run_pipeline_task.delay",
            return_value=mock_celery_result
        \)

        # Trigger pipeline
        response = await client.post\(
            "/api/v1/pipeline",
            json={
                "topic": "5 facts about space",
                "video_format": "short",
                "provider": "openai",
                "skip_upload": False
            }
        \)

        # Verify response
        assert response.status_code == 202
        data = response.json\(\)
        assert "project_id" in data
        assert data["celery_task_id"] == "celery-task-123"
        assert data["status"] == "pending"
        assert "Poll" in data["message"]

        # Verify Celery task was dispatched
        mock_delay.assert_called_once\(\)
        call_kwargs = mock_delay.call_args[1]
        assert call_kwargs["topic"] == "5 facts about space"
        assert call_kwargs["video_format"] == "short"
        assert call_kwargs["provider"] == "openai"
        assert call_kwargs["skip_upload"] is False

        # Verify project was created in database
        from sqlalchemy import select
        result = await db_session.execute\(select\(VideoProject\)\)
        projects = result.scalars\(\).all\(\)
        assert len\(projects\) == 1
        assert projects[0].topic == "5 facts about space"
        assert projects[0].status == VideoStatus.PENDING

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_trigger_pipeline_invalid_topic_too_short\(self, client: AsyncClient\):
        """Test that topics shorter than 3 chars are rejected."""
        response = await client.post\(
            "/api/v1/pipeline",
            json={
                "topic": "ab",  # Too short
                "video_format": "short"
            }
        \)

        assert response.status_code == 422  # Validation error

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_trigger_pipeline_invalid_topic_too_long\(self, client: AsyncClient\):
        """Test that topics longer than 512 chars are rejected."""
        response = await client.post\(
            "/api/v1/pipeline",
            json={
                "topic": "a" * 513,  # Too long
                "video_format": "short"
            }
        \)

        assert response.status_code == 422

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_trigger_pipeline_invalid_format\(self, client: AsyncClient\):
        """Test that invalid video formats are rejected."""
        response = await client.post\(
            "/api/v1/pipeline",
            json={
                "topic": "Test topic",
                "video_format": "invalid_format"
            }
        \)

        assert response.status_code == 422

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_trigger_pipeline_invalid_provider\(self, client: AsyncClient\):
        """Test that invalid LLM providers are rejected."""
        response = await client.post\(
            "/api/v1/pipeline",
            json={
                "topic": "Test topic",
                "provider": "invalid_provider"
            }
        \)

        assert response.status_code == 422

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_trigger_pipeline_celery_failure\(self, client: AsyncClient, db_session, mocker\):
        """Test that Celery dispatch failures are handled gracefully."""
        # Mock Celery task to raise exception
        mocker.patch\(
            "app.api.routes.pipeline.run_pipeline_task.delay",
            side_effect=Exception\("Redis connection failed"\)
        \)

        response = await client.post\(
            "/api/v1/pipeline",
            json={
                "topic": "Test topic",
                "video_format": "short"
            }
        \)

        # Should return 503 Service Unavailable
        assert response.status_code == 503
        assert "Redis" in response.json\(\)["detail"]

        # Verify project was created but marked as FAILED
        from sqlalchemy import select
        result = await db_session.execute\(select\(VideoProject\)\)
        projects = result.scalars\(\).all\(\)
        assert len\(projects\) == 1
        assert projects[0].status == VideoStatus.FAILED
        assert "Celery dispatch failed" in projects[0].error_message

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_trigger_pipeline_defaults\(self, client: AsyncClient, mocker\):
        """Test default values for optional fields."""
        mock_celery_result = MagicMock\(\)
        mock_celery_result.id = "task-123"

        mock_delay = mocker.patch\(
            "app.api.routes.pipeline.run_pipeline_task.delay",
            return_value=mock_celery_result
        \)

        response = await client.post\(
            "/api/v1/pipeline",
            json={"topic": "Test topic"}  # Only required field
        \)

        assert response.status_code == 202

        # Verify defaults
        call_kwargs = mock_delay.call_args[1]
        assert call_kwargs["video_format"] == "short"  # Default
        assert call_kwargs["provider"] == "openai"  # Default
        assert call_kwargs["skip_upload"] is False  # Default

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_trigger_pipeline_with_anthropic\(self, client: AsyncClient, mocker\):
        """Test pipeline trigger with Anthropic provider."""
        mock_celery_result = MagicMock\(\)
        mock_celery_result.id = "task-123"

        mock_delay = mocker.patch\(
            "app.api.routes.pipeline.run_pipeline_task.delay",
            return_value=mock_celery_result
        \)

        response = await client.post\(
            "/api/v1/pipeline",
            json={
                "topic": "Test topic",
                "provider": "anthropic"
            }
        \)

        assert response.status_code == 202

        call_kwargs = mock_delay.call_args[1]
        assert call_kwargs["provider"] == "anthropic"


class TestBatchPipeline:
    """Test POST /api/v1/pipeline/batch endpoint."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_batch_pipeline_success\(self, client: AsyncClient, db_session, mocker\):
        """Test successful batch pipeline trigger."""
        mock_celery_result = MagicMock\(\)
        mock_celery_result.id = "task-batch-123"

        mocker.patch\(
            "app.api.routes.pipeline.run_pipeline_task.delay",
            return_value=mock_celery_result
        \)

        response = await client.post\(
            "/api/v1/pipeline/batch",
            json=[
                {"topic": "Topic 1", "video_format": "short"},
                {"topic": "Topic 2", "video_format": "long"},
                {"topic": "Topic 3"}
            ]
        \)

        assert response.status_code == 202
        data = response.json\(\)
        assert len\(data\) == 3
        assert all\(item["status"] == "pending" for item in data\)

        # Verify all projects created
        from sqlalchemy import select
        result = await db_session.execute\(select\(VideoProject\)\)
        projects = result.scalars\(\).all\(\)
        assert len\(projects\) == 3

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_batch_pipeline_exceeds_limit\(self, client: AsyncClient\):
        """Test that batches over 10 requests are rejected."""
        requests = [{"topic": f"Topic {i}"} for i in range\(11\)]

        response = await client.post\(
            "/api/v1/pipeline/batch",
            json=requests
        \)

        assert response.status_code == 400
        assert "Batch limit is 10" in response.json\(\)["detail"]

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_batch_pipeline_partial_failure\(self, client: AsyncClient, db_session, mocker\):
        """Test that batch continues even if some dispatches fail."""
        call_count = [0]

        def mock_delay\(**kwargs\):
            call_count[0] += 1
            if call_count[0] == 2:
                # Second request fails
                raise Exception\("Celery error"\)
            result = MagicMock\(\)
            result.id = f"task-{call_count[0]}"
            return result

        mocker.patch\(
            "app.api.routes.pipeline.run_pipeline_task.delay",
            side_effect=mock_delay
        \)

        response = await client.post\(
            "/api/v1/pipeline/batch",
            json=[
                {"topic": "Topic 1"},
                {"topic": "Topic 2"},  # This will fail
                {"topic": "Topic 3"}
            ]
        \)

        assert response.status_code == 202
        data = response.json\(\)
        assert len\(data\) == 3
        assert data[0]["status"] == "pending"
        assert data[1]["status"] == "failed"  # Failed
        assert data[2]["status"] == "pending"

        # Verify all projects created
        from sqlalchemy import select
        result = await db_session.execute\(select\(VideoProject\)\)
        projects = result.scalars\(\).all\(\)
        assert len\(projects\) == 3

        # Check statuses
        statuses = {p.topic: p.status for p in projects}
        assert statuses["Topic 1"] == VideoStatus.PENDING
        assert statuses["Topic 2"] == VideoStatus.FAILED
        assert statuses["Topic 3"] == VideoStatus.PENDING
INTEGRATION_TEST_EOF)

from app.services.tts_service import _preprocess_text_for_tts

# Test for double-space bug in currency without magnitude
tests = [
    \(''He earned $500 today.'', ''currency_no_magnitude''\),
    \(''It costs $2.5 per unit.'', ''currency_decimal_no_magnitude''\),
    \(''Worth $2.5M easily.'', ''currency_with_magnitude''\),
    \(''About 99.9% of cases.'', ''percentage''\),
    \(''He finished 1st in the race.'', ''ordinal''\),
    \(''Going 60 mph on the highway.'', ''units''\),
    \(''In 2024 something happened.'', ''year_modern''\),
    \(''There are 6000000 stars.'', ''large_number''\),
    \(''There are 5 reasons.'', ''small_number''\),
    \(''The US government.'', ''acronym''\),
    \(''The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.'', ''plain_text''\),
    \(''Back in 1776 the nation formed.'', ''historical_year''\),
    \(''The temperature was 37°C outside.'', ''degrees''\),
    \(''She ran 5 km in 30 minutes.'', ''mixed_units''\),
    \(''NASA spent $2.5B on the ISS project.'', ''complex''\),
]

for text, label in tests:
    result = _preprocess_text_for_tts\(text\)
    has_double_space = ''  '' in result
    marker = '' !! DOUBLE SPACE !!'' if has_double_space else ''''
    print\(f''{label}: {result!r}{marker}''\)
")
